evidence.text: Briefly, 73M with CAD s/p CABG, DM, ESRD on HD, Afib on coumadin    admitted to the neurology service [**8-12**] after being transferred from    [**Hospital3 1492**] for further management of a traumatic L SDH sustained 3    days prior to admission.

evidence.text: Has had epileptiform behavior for which    receiving continuous EEG monitoring and ativan boluses (1 mg IV x 3    over past 36 hours).

evidence.text: Had PR temp 102.5 yesterday evening. 30 minutes    into HD session today had BP 70/23.

evidence.text: CODE:  FULL    ACCESS:  Right double lumen PICC, Left HD cath    SOCIAL: Contact is with daughter [**Name (NI) **] [**Telephone/Fax (1) 8525**] who works in the    [**Hospital 4486**] Medical Office Building.

evidence.display: ROS:    *neuro- A&Ox1 (person) and able to follow commands at times;    moans/gr
evidence.text: ROS:    *neuro- A&Ox1 (person) and able to follow commands at times;    moans/groans/yells out but when asked if in pain denies; pupils    unequal, non reactive (please see below)    *resp- satting 94-98% on 6L NC with RR 10s-20s; LS ronchorous    throughout, congested cough present    *CV- HR 80s SR, no ectopy; BPs ranging 100s-140s systolic; BP being    taken on right calf as unable to take on BUE [**12-20**] PICC on right and old    AV fistula on left    *GI/[**Name (NI) 2861**] pt does not make urine; HD pt M/W/F, has NGT in place to right    nare with Nutren Pulmonary Full infusing @ 10cc/hr (starting rate), tol    without N/V, abd S/NT/slightly distended with +BS; flexiseal in place    draining brown liquid stool    *act- moves all extremities on bed except RUE; when extremities lifted    up, they fall back as pt is unable to lift and hold; did wiggle toes at    times on command    *endo- FSBS 148 @ 1200, 147 @ 1800 requiring no insulin coverage per    SS; also as fixed dose which was not given this am [**12-20**] NPO, but will be    needed @ bedtime    Fever, unknown origin (FUO, Hyperthermia, Pyrexia)    Assessment:    Pt with tmax 102.5 rectal on floor; transferred to MICU for fever with    hypotension suggesting possible sepsis.
Tmax 99.5 today.
